qa_pairs.question: In 2023, the UK is providing some households with a £900 payment to help with the cost of living. It will arrive as three payments of £301, £300, and £299, respectively. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: So people can easily tell which payment they have received or missed; the differing amounts act as identifiers for the three instalments.

qa_pairs.question: Each summer, people flock to a Californian tourist attraction to have their photo taken next to two numbers. In 2021, a coveted pair of numbers was 57 and 135. What's happening?
qa_pairs.answer: People are taking selfies with the Death Valley temperature sign when it shows extreme heat: 57°C and 135°F.

qa_pairs.question: Anna is a performer. Every few days, she needs to do something with a couple of lengths of dental floss. Why does she do this, and what's her job?
qa_pairs.answer: She is a ballet dancer, and she uses dental floss to sew ribbons and elastics onto her pointe shoes because it is stronger than thread; she has to redo it every few days because the shoes wear out quickly.

qa_pairs.question: In 1854, a man was on a raised platform in front of a crowd. He ordered an axeman to cut the only rope holding him up. Not only did he survive, but he became a leading figure in his industry. Which industry is that?
qa_pairs.answer: The elevator industry; Elisha Otis was demonstrating the safety brake on his passenger elevator.

qa_pairs.question: In the 1960s, '70s, and '80s, there were dozens of hugely expensive, state-of-the-art US satellites that ceased operation after no more than a few weeks in orbit. Even though they were still fully controllable and spaceworthy at the time, they quickly became useless junk. Why?
qa_pairs.answer: They were spy satellites that used photographic film; once they had exposed and de-orbited all their film canisters, the satellites had nothing left to do and were burned up.

qa_pairs.question: Many of the oldest houses on the small Greek island of Ikaria have no chimneys and low ceilings. Many of them are also built under rocks. Why is this?
qa_pairs.answer: They were built to hide from pirates, blending into the landscape and avoiding visible smoke from chimneys.

qa_pairs.question: Why are places where you can buy books, pens, and paper called stationery shops?
qa_pairs.answer: Because books were heavy, sellers of them tended to stay in one regular place rather than travel, so they were known as stationers.

qa_pairs.question: What eventually contains 178 feet, 81 heads, 46 wings, and eight udders?
qa_pairs.answer: The song "The Twelve Days of Christmas."
